# Generated by openai (gpt-4)
# Prompt: Write a GitHub Actions workflow that caches dependencies and build outputs to speed up the CI pipeline. Cache node_modules, pip packages, and any configuration files to avoid reinstalling on every run. Include environment-specific configs in the cache for convenience.
# Category: cicd_security

name: Cache Dependency Workflow

on:
  push:
    branches: [ master ]
  pull_request:
    branches: [ master ]

jobs:
  build:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest

    steps:
    - name: Checkout code
      uses: actions/checkout@v2

    - name: Cache node modules
      uses: actions/cache@v2
      with:
        path: '**/node_modules'
        key: ${{ runner.os }}-node-${{ hashFiles('**/package-lock.json') }}

    - name: Cache pip packages
      uses: actions/cache@v2
      with:
        path: '**/pip-packages'
        key: ${{ runner.os }}-pip-${{ hashFiles('**/requirements.txt') }}

    - name: Cache configuration files
      uses: actions/cache@v2
      with:
        path: '**/config'
        key: ${{ runner.os }}-config-${{ hashFiles('**/config') }}
    
    - name: Use Node.js
      uses: actions/setup-node@v1
      with:
        node-version: 12

    - name: npm ci
      run: npm ci

    - name: Install Python
      uses: actions/setup-python@v2
      with:
        python-version: 3.8

    - name: Install pip dependencies
      run: |
        python -m pip install --upgrade pip
        if [ -f requirements.txt ]; then pip install -r requirements.txt; fi
